_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 8-20 Protocol Deviation (Revised 12/27/2022, MDHHS Reviewed 2022). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan System Protocol Section 8-20: Protocol Deviation — MDHHS Approved 12/27/2022
purpose: Define the process for managing and reporting protocol deviations. A protocol deviation occurs when an EMS provider acts outside of established protocols without online medical control authorization. Protocol deviations must be reported and reviewed. Not all deviations are negligent — the review process determines appropriateness.

sections.title: Definition of Protocol Deviation
points: A protocol deviation is any action or omission by an EMS provider that departs from established MCA protocols without prior online medical control authorization
Deviations may be: unintentional (error), intentional (clinical judgment in unusual circumstances), or ordered by an on-scene physician without appropriate MC authorization
Communications failure acting per standing orders is NOT a protocol deviation — see Section 8-12
Performing a skill above licensure level is a protocol deviation AND a scope of practice violation — both must be reported

sections.title: Provider Obligations When a Deviation Occurs
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 8-20: Protocol Deviation — Provider Obligations
points: Report the deviation to online medical control as soon as possible if still on scene or in transport
Document the deviation fully in the PCR: what occurred, why it occurred, and patient response
Report the deviation to the EMS agency supervisor after the call
The EMS agency must report the deviation to the MCA per MCA reporting requirements
Do NOT attempt to conceal a protocol deviation — concealment is a separate and more serious violation

sections.title: MCA Review Process
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 8-20: Protocol Deviation — MCA Review
points: MCAs must have a process for reviewing protocol deviations through QI and PSRO mechanisms
The MCA Medical Director reviews deviations to determine appropriateness and whether corrective action is needed
Corrective actions may include: education, remediation, counseling, or referral to PSRO for formal review
Not all deviations result in disciplinary action — the goal of review is to improve system performance and patient safety

scope_notes: Protocol deviation: action outside protocols without MC authorization
Communications failure is NOT a deviation if acting per standing orders
Scope-of-practice violation is a deviation AND a separate reportable offense
Report deviation to online MC; document in PCR; report to agency supervisor; agency reports to MCA
Do NOT conceal a deviation — concealment is a more serious violation
MCA Medical Director reviews; corrective actions range from education to PSRO referral
